text: Greater peak hip adduction is associated with developing patellofemoral pain and iliotibial band syndrome in female runners.
The association between peak hip adduction and running-related injuries does not hold consistently in mixed-sex populations of cross-country runners.
The relationship between vertical loading rate and running-related injuries is inconsistent across prospective studies.
No single biomechanical variable has been consistently identified as a reliable predictor of running-related injuries across populations.
Biomechanical risk factors for running injuries are highly population-specific and injury-specific, meaning risk profiles differ between sexes, running types, and injury locations.
